design_philosophy.core_loop: Hit energy ball around a 3D room to destroy wall tiles and navigate through maze rooms; manage ball control, enemy encounters, and limited ammunition
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Preventing ball from going behind the glove (loss condition) while destroying required tiles to progress; exploration vs. getting lost in large mazes
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Controlling a virtual glove in 3D space to solve spatial puzzles and clear physics-based tile destruction rooms; rescuing a shuttle commander from a dimensional maze
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Explicitly designed around Power Glove controller (playable with standard NES controller as alternative); emphasizes exploration and manual maze mapping as 'practically half the fun'; tile-based room destruction is the primary interaction model rather than combat or platforming

difficulty.lives_system: 26-bar life meter; lose one life after losing 5 energy balls; finite lives with no continues
difficulty.continues_system: No continues; game over upon losing all lives
difficulty.checkpointing: None documented; player must navigate from start of each maze
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Hidden level select available via cheat code (Up, Left, Down, A, Right, A, B, A on standard controller); progressive difficulty across three worlds with tougher enemies and harder-to-hit tiles

progression.upgrade_system: Temporary power-ups from question mark tiles: LIFE, EXTRA, ICE BALL, BOMB BALL, POWER, ROW, SUPER BALL, TRIPLE, ROBO-BULLET REFILL, PINBALL; Robo-Bullets (finite ammo, max 99)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Clearing all tiles on a wall opens red arrow path to adjacent rooms in maze; PINBALL power-up opens all pathways regardless of tile destruction
replayability_hooks: Three separate mazes with hundreds of rooms each (second world alone has 60+ rooms)
High score list (resets on power cycle)
Random room warp mechanic creates unpredictable exploration

danger_signals: Certain tiles much harder to hit in worlds 2 and 3
Some enemies deal instant life loss (one-hit KO)
environmental_storytelling: 3D room architecture with tiered wall surfaces creates spatial puzzle narrative
Maze structure requires manual mapping and cognitive engagement
Question mark tiles introduce discovery mechanic
